channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Quality of Life & PROs
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Quality of Life & PROs

item.title: Efficacy and safety of ulcerative colitis therapies do not differ by age: A post hoc analysis of randomized controlled trials.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Older adults with ulcerative colitis (UC) are often undertreated with advanced therapies due to safety concerns. We evaluated whether outcomes differ by age in randomized trials. Post hoc analysis ...

item.title: Reinterpreting symptom profiles and network centrality in inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Digestive and liver disease : official journal of the Italian Society of Gastroenterology and the Italian Association for the Study of the Liver - Letter -

item.title: Surface-Engineered Titanium Carbide MXenzyme Boosts ROS-Scavenging and Anti-Inflammation Activities for Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: ACS applied materials & interfaces - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is persistent or recurrent intestinal inflammation. The severity of IBD is highly associated with an imbalance between M1 and M2 macrophages. In this study, a novel...

item.title: Sleep Architecture Screening in Hospitalized Patients with Inflammatory Bowel Diseases: A Prospective Propensity Score-Matched Analysis.
item.description: Clinical and translational gastroenterology - To evaluate the effectiveness of sleep architecture screening in hospitalized patients with inflammatory bowel diseases (IBDs) from the viewpoint of long-term survival from IBDs worsening. A total ...

item.title: The Multiple Dimensions of Self-Management in Active Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Qualitative Analysis of Patients' Daily Lived Experiences.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Self-management is critical to improving inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) health but challenging for patients. To inform the development of IBD self-management interventions and improve IBD health,...

item.title: Understanding Patient Preferences on Inflammatory Bowel Disease Treatment Modalities and Outcomes: A Global Targeted Literature Review.
item.description: Advances in therapy - Review - Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBDs) are chronic diseases, including Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC). Given the heterogeneity in disease presentation, treatment responses, and lifest...

item.title: Food-related quality of life in inflammatory bowel disease: validity and reliability of the Brazilian version of the FR-QoL-29-Portuguese-Brazil.
item.description: Gastroenterologia y hepatologia - Our aim was to (i) undertake a comprehensive translation of the FR-QoL-29 instrument to Brazilian-Portuguese; and (ii) evaluate its validity and reliability in patients with IBD in Brazil. The FR-Q...

item.title: Letter: The Persistence of Anxiety and Depression in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Core Phenotype Beyond Inflammatory Activity.
The association of psychological distress with microbiota alterations in patients suffering from quiescent inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Journal of psychosomatic research - The role of the gut microbiota and brain-gut axis in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), a group of multifactorial chronic disorders of uncertain etiology, is receiving increasing attention. However,...

item.title: CBP phosphorylation maintains intestinal homeostasis by supporting the stem cell niche through versican.
item.description: Nature communications - CREB-binding protein (CBP) is a transcriptional coactivator, and we reported its phosphorylation regulates cell fate. Impaired CBP phosphorylation alters its binding preference for p53 in CBPAA mic...

item.title: The Protein Tyrosine Phosphatase CD45 promotes PMN Transepithelial Migration, Antimicrobial Function and Colonic Mucosal Repair.
item.description: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology - Polymorphonuclear neutrophils (PMNs) serve as frontline defenders against injury and infection, eliminating pathogens and initiating mucosal tissue repair. However, excessive PMN transepithelial mi...

item.title: Child and parent perspectives in IBD management: a literature review and qualitative study.
item.description: Crohn's & colitis 360 - The therapeutic landscape and treatment goals for pediatric inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) continue to evolve. However, there remains a paucity of literature capturing the experiences and prefere...
